cycle_label: GOV-06 — governance-thread standalone sprint · ops-dashboard doc-health data-path fix + freshness watch + process-health tracking pass · runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade

plain_english._doc: Operator-facing summary block. /brief reads this to emit a plain-language header. The governance thread is NOT a cycle — it is a thread of standalone GOV-NN sprints. cycle_number tracks the GOV sprint number (GOV-06 = 6). Re-author this block at each GOV sprint OPEN.
plain_english.summary: The governance thread runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade — not a 4th nephew. It scopes from a health-check sweep, takes ownerless cross-cutting work the cascade structurally can't absorb, and codifies findings to memory + DAcumen. GOV-06 opened 2026-05-14, operator-directed from a targeted sweep of the /ops dashboard. L01's diagnostic sweep found the root cause of the busted doc-health panel: the /pipeline-api/ prod proxy was deprecated 2026-05-13, but DocHealth.vue was never reconciled — it still fetches the dead path, so the prod panel shows 'Pipeline unavailable'. The doc-health pipeline runs dev-only. Separately, L01 established that process-health 57 is NOT a bug — the panel honestly reports that commit-to-deployment tracking is behind. GOV-06 fixes the doc-health data path the canonical way (static artifact under /observatory/data/, same pattern as telemetry-contract-status.json), adds a freshness watch so it can't silently rot again, and does a process-health tracking pass to lift evidence-coverage. L01 closed; L02 (doc-health fix) / L03 (freshness watch) / L04 (process-health pass + close) pending.
goals: L01 — ops-dashboard diagnostic sweep. DONE. Root cause of the busted doc-health panel: /pipeline-api/ prod proxy deprecated 2026-05-13 (returns HTTP 404 + deprecation JSON), DocHealth.vue never reconciled — still fetches the dead path → 'Pipeline unavailable'. The doc-health pipeline is healthy but dev-only (dev casey-pipeline :8912 scans local files, returns 47 projects avg 0.697; prod casey-junior has the route but returns empty). process-health 57 confirmed NOT a bug — /api/reconciliation/health honestly reports evidence_coverage 58 (1251/2166 commits mapped) + traceability_depth 83. Fix pattern identified: static artifact under /observatory/data/, the same path telemetry-contract-status.json uses.

L02 — doc-health panel fix. DONE. New darntech/scripts/doc-health-snapshot.sh curls the dev pipeline → writes observatory/data/doc-health-status.json → deploys; nightly 23:50 systemd --user timer installed; DocHealth.vue repointed from the dead /pipeline-api/ to the static artifact (canonical pattern, mirrors telemetry-contract-status.json). No casey-junior change needed — the dev pipeline already serves the data; L02 work_locus collapsed to darntech-only. Built clean, deployed to CT 100, verified on prod: artifact HTTP 200 (47 projects, avg 0.697), dashboard bundle matches the dev build. Commit da94904.

L03 — doc-health freshness watch. PENDING. Standing checker (systemd --user timer) that goes failed if doc-health-status.json is missing or stale — same shape as health-refresh-check + observatory-telemetry-contract-check, so a rotted artifact lands where a GOV sweep already greps.
L04 — process-health pass + GOV-06 close. PENDING. Register unmapped repos in casey-junior PROJECT_ENDPOINTS to lift evidence_coverage; re-check the composite score; close GOV-06.
plain_english.proposed_next_action: GOV-06 L01 + L02 are CLOSED — the doc-health panel is fixed, deployed, and prod-verified (static artifact at /observatory/data/doc-health-status.json, nightly 23:50 timer keeps it fresh). Proceed to L03: add a doc-health-artifact freshness watch — a systemd --user checker that goes `failed` if doc-health-status.json is missing or stale, same shape as health-refresh-check + observatory-telemetry-contract-check, so a rotted artifact lands where a GOV sweep already greps. Then L04: process-health tracking pass — register unmapped repos in casey-junior PROJECT_ENDPOINTS to lift evidence_coverage (1251/2166 commits mapped at open), re-check the composite score, close GOV-06.

cycle_mode_notes: GOV sprints do not pillar-rotate and do not join the cascade. cascade_mode is standalone by design — the three-sprint cascade is capped at 3 (three is the floor AND the ceiling). GOV takes ownerless cross-cutting work, never nephew COLLECT-queue work.
structure: Health-check sweep scopes the queue → GOV executes the ownerless backlog as a standalone sprint → findings codified to memory + DAcumen
cascade_mode: standalone — runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade, not a 4th nephew
authored_by: operator-direct — GOV-06 opened immediately after GOV-05 closed. Operator handed GOV a targeted symptom (the /ops doc-health panel is busted, process-health stuck at 57); L01's diagnostic sweep ran same-session and found the root cause — a deprecation that stranded a consumer. Operator-confirmed scope: doc-health data-path fix + freshness watch + process-health tracking pass.
stakes: The /ops dashboard is the single source of truth for project status — a busted doc-health panel means a core ops surface is silently lying. The root cause is the cascade-rc-rename-consumer-runtime-gap pattern again: a deprecation (the /pipeline-api/ prod proxy, 2026-05-13) was not 'landed' because its consumer (DocHealth.vue) was never reconciled. GOV-06 fixes the data path onto the canonical static-artifact pattern AND adds a freshness watch — so the panel both works now and reports its own failure if the artifact rots, rather than silently showing stale or empty data.
notes: GOV-06 opened 2026-05-14, operator-directed, work_locus darntech + casey-junior. Scope: (1) doc-health pipeline writes observatory/data/doc-health-status.json + DocHealth.vue reads the static artifact (canonical pattern, mirrors telemetry-contract-status.json / TelemetryContractsCard.vue); (2) standing freshness watch for the artifact; (3) process-health tracking pass — register unmapped repos in PROJECT_ENDPOINTS to lift evidence_coverage (1251/2166 commits mapped at open). Key durable-finding candidate: deprecating a prod proxy without reconciling its dashboard consumers strands the consumer silently — the deprecation response was a clean 404+JSON, so nothing alerted; the dashboard just showed 'unavailable'. Deploy to dev AND prod per homelab rules. Governance-thread workspace remains a checkout of dacumen.git; Casey Junior deployment 09c22323.
